meta_title: Impact Doors in Plantation Acres, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Protect your Plantation Acres home with hurricane-rated impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. ESW & CWI brands. Licensed, local, A+ BBB. Free estimates — call today!
h1: Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors Built for Plantation Acres Homes
intro:
Plantation Acres is one of Broward County's most distinctive residential communities — a rare blend of sprawling estate-style properties, equestrian lots, and lush landscaping that gives homeowners a sense of space and serenity that's hard to find anywhere else in South Florida. With average home values sitting around $550,000 and properties throughout Plantation Acres Estates reflecting generations of investment and care, protecting your home from South Florida's relentless storm season isn't a luxury — it's a responsibility. Impact doors are one of the most powerful upgrades a homeowner in this community can make, offering protection that standard doors simply cannot provide when hurricane-force winds arrive.

Located approximately 9 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Plantation Acres falls within Broward County's 170 mph design wind speed zone. While the area sits just outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) designation that applies to Miami-Dade and coastal Broward, the Florida Building Code still mandates hurricane-rated products for all new installations and replacements. That means your entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors must meet strict performance standards — and cutting corners isn't just a safety risk, it's a code violation that can complicate insurance claims and future home sales. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing ensures every door we install in Plantation Acres is fully compliant, properly permitted, and built to withstand whatever the Atlantic throws at this community.

For households near Plantation Acres Park and throughout the quiet cul-de-sacs and private drives that define this neighborhood, the right impact door does more than deflect wind and debris. It seals out noise, reduces energy costs, and adds a layer of security that standard doors with single-point locks simply can't match. Whether you're upgrading a grand front entry, replacing aging sliding glass doors that face a backyard pool, or installing French doors on a sunroom addition, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — has the product selection, technical expertise, and local track record to do it right the first time.

section_why.h2: Why Plantation Acres Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Door Installations
section_why.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we don't subcontract. Every impact door installation in Plantation Acres is handled entirely by our own trained, licensed crews — people who show up in Premier Impact Windows & Roofing trucks, wear Premier Impact Windows & Roofing uniforms, and are accountable to our company's standards from start to finish. That commitment to 100% in-house installation is something most competitors can't claim, and it makes a measurable difference in the quality and consistency of every project we complete. With over 60 years of combined field experience across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, our installation teams have seen every door configuration, framing condition, and structural challenge that South Florida homes present.

Plantation Acres homes range widely in age and architecture — from mid-century ranch-style builds to newer custom estates — and our teams are well-versed in handling the unique rough opening conditions, threshold heights, and framing materials associated with each. Nearly 40% of homes in this area were built before 1994, meaning they predate the post-Andrew building code reforms that transformed South Florida construction standards. Older doors on these properties are especially vulnerable, and replacing them with properly rated impact units is often the single most impactful upgrade an owner can make. We assess each opening individually and recommend the right product for the specific conditions of your home — not just what's easiest to install.

We've completed more than 500 homes across South Florida and hold an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, a reflection of our commitment to doing things right. Our process includes pulling all required permits through the Broward County Building Division, scheduling required inspections, and providing homeowners with all the documentation they need for insurance purposes and future resale disclosures. From your first call to the final inspection sign-off, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every detail so you don't have to. That's what it means to be The Kings of Service in Plantation Acres.

section_history.h2: Plantation Acres Has Faced 9 Named Storms Since 1990 — Here's What That History Teaches Us
section_history.body:
The storm history of Broward County reads like a cautionary tale for any homeowner who has delayed upgrading their doors and windows. Plantation Acres and the surrounding communities of Plantation, Davie, and Sunrise have been tested repeatedly by major Atlantic hurricanes. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a defining moment for South Florida, exposing catastrophic failures in home construction standards and triggering a complete overhaul of the Florida Building Code. Homes in Broward County that lacked proper hurricane protection suffered significant structural damage, and many of those lessons are still relevant today — especially for the 40% of Plantation Acres homes built before those reforms took effect.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 struck as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, and its rapid traverse across South Florida caught many Broward residents off guard. Wilma's forward speed and sustained winds caused widespread damage to doors, particularly sliding glass doors and aging front entry systems that lacked impact-resistant glazing or multi-point locking hardware. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which served as another reminder that even storms that don't score a direct hit can generate sustained winds and tornado-embedded gusts that overwhelm standard residential doors. Plantation Acres experienced downed trees, power outages, and structural damage from Irma's outer bands — and homes without impact-rated doors bore the brunt of that damage.

With nine named storms affecting this area since 1990, the question for Plantation Acres homeowners isn't whether another significant hurricane will impact Broward County — it's whether your doors will hold when it does. Impact doors are not just rated for wind pressure; they're designed to resist windborne debris, which is the primary mechanism of door failure during major storms. Investing in properly installed impact doors today is a decision that pays dividends every hurricane season.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Broward County
section_code.body:
Plantation Acres falls under Broward County jurisdiction for building permits, which means all door replacements must comply with Florida Building Code (FBC) requirements and be permitted through the Broward County Building Division. While this area sits just outside the formal High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) boundary — a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and portions of coastal Broward — the FBC still requires that all exterior doors in this wind zone be rated for a minimum design wind speed of 170 mph. This applies to impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors alike. Products must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Product Approval from a recognized Florida testing body, and installation must follow the exact specifications outlined in that approval.

Permit requirements in Broward County mean that unpermitted door replacements — even when using technically compliant products — can create serious legal and financial exposure for homeowners. If a storm causes damage and your carrier discovers that your doors were replaced without permits, your claim may be denied or significantly reduced. Worse, unpermitted work can hold up a real estate transaction and require costly remediation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting on your behalf, submitting the required product documentation, installation drawings, and contractor credentials to the Broward County Building Division before a single tool is unpackaged at your home.

All ESW and CWI doors we install in Plantation Acres carry the Florida Product Approvals required for Broward County compliance, and our installation crews follow manufacturer-specified anchor patterns and sealing requirements to the letter. When the required inspections are complete and the permit is closed, you receive a fully documented, code-compliant installation — the kind that satisfies insurance carriers, home inspectors, and future buyers alike. Compliance isn't a formality at Premier Impact Windows & Roofing; it's built into every step of our process.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Products Engineered for Plantation Acres Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of South Florida's most respected manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands produce product lines specifically engineered and tested to meet Florida's demanding wind and impact requirements, and both carry the Florida Product Approvals necessary for permitted installation in Broward County. Whether you're looking for a bold, custom-finished entry door that makes a statement on your Plantation Acres estate, or a sleek sliding glass door system that maximizes your backyard views without compromising storm protection, these brands offer the combination of performance and aesthetics that upper-tier homes in this community demand.

ESW impact entry doors are constructed with reinforced frames and multi-point locking systems that engage multiple deadbolts simultaneously along the full height of the door, dramatically increasing resistance to both wind pressure and forced entry. Their impact-laminated glass units are tested to large-missile impact standards — meaning they can withstand the kind of airborne debris that travels at lethal velocities in a 170 mph wind event. ESW's sliding glass door systems are equally impressive, featuring heavy-duty rollers, interlocking panels, and continuous perimeter seals that maintain their integrity under extreme pressure differentials. For homeowners who prefer the classic architectural appeal of French doors, both ESW and CWI offer multi-panel configurations with flush bolts, shoot bolts, and active-panel multi-point locks that provide genuine hurricane protection without sacrificing elegance.

CWI doors are equally well-suited to Plantation Acres homes, offering a broad range of frame finishes, glass options, and decorative configurations that complement the architectural diversity of this community. Both brands provide excellent thermal performance, with insulated frames and Low-E glass options that can meaningfully reduce cooling loads in South Florida's subtropical climate. When you invest in an ESW or CWI impact door through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're getting a product line that has been tested, approved, and proven in the field — installed by professionals who know exactly how to deliver the performance those ratings promise.

section_cost.h2: What Do Impact Doors Cost for Plantation Acres Homes — And How Do You Finance Them?
section_cost.body:
For a home valued at $550,000 in Plantation Acres Estates, the cost of impact door replacement is a meaningful investment — but one that delivers measurable returns in protection, energy savings, insurance discounts, and resale value. The exact cost depends on the number and type of doors being replaced, the size of each opening, the product line selected, and any structural modifications required at the rough opening. A single impact entry door installation in a Plantation Acres home typically ranges from $2,500 to $5,500 installed, while sliding glass door systems — especially larger multi-panel configurations common in estate properties — can range from $4,000 to $10,000 or more. Full-home door packages that include entry, sliding, and French doors are often completed at a combined cost that reflects meaningful per-unit savings compared to piecemeal replacement.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options that make it possible to move forward without depleting savings or waiting for the next storm to force the decision. Our financing programs start as low as $87 per month, with $0 down options available to qualified applicants. For homeowners who want to replace all their doors — or combine a door project with impact window installation — financing allows you to protect your entire home now and pay over time. Many Plantation Acres homeowners find that the monthly insurance savings they realize after installation offset a significant portion of their financing cost, making the net out-of-pocket impact far smaller than the sticker price suggests.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors consistently rank among the highest-value home improvements in South Florida's real estate market. Buyers in the $500,000-plus price tier in Broward County actively seek homes with impact protection already in place, and properties with fully documented impact door and window installations often command premiums that exceed the original installation cost. Beyond resale, you're also protecting a $550,000 asset from storm damage that can easily cost tens of thousands of dollars — or more — in a single event. The math strongly favors acting sooner rather than later.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance Premiums in Plantation Acres
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County has become increasingly expensive as carriers recalibrate their risk models for South Florida's hurricane exposure. For Plantation Acres homeowners, impact doors represent one of the most direct and reliable ways to reduce insurance costs. Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts for wind mitigation features that have been verified through a licensed wind mitigation inspection, and impact-rated doors — when properly installed and documented — qualify for significant credits under the mitigation rating system. The exact savings vary by carrier and policy, but many Plantation Acres homeowners report annual premium reductions of $500 to $1,500 or more after completing a full impact door and window installation.

The key to capturing these savings is documentation. When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your installation, we provide you with the product NOA numbers, permit records, and inspection sign-offs that a wind mitigation inspector needs to verify your upgraded protection. A post-installation wind mitigation inspection — typically performed by a licensed inspector for $100 to $200 — then produces a report that you submit to your insurance carrier to trigger the rate adjustment. Major carriers active in Broward County, including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, and several others, all have established wind mitigation discount schedules that reward homes with impact-rated openings.

It's worth noting that the insurance benefit extends beyond the annual premium discount. In the event of a storm, a home with properly installed impact doors is far less likely to suffer the kind of catastrophic water intrusion that results from door failure — the type of damage that triggers large claims, rate surcharges, and in some cases, policy non-renewal. Impact doors reduce your risk profile in the eyes of your carrier, and in today's tightening South Florida insurance market, that matters more than ever. Protecting your Plantation Acres home with impact doors is as much a financial strategy as it is a safety decision.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Plantation Acres if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. While Plantation Acres is approximately 9 miles from the Atlantic coast, the Florida Building Code mandates hurricane-rated doors for all of Broward County based on a 170 mph design wind speed. Storm-generated winds and windborne debris don't discriminate by distance from the shoreline — hurricanes like Irma and Wilma caused significant interior Broward damage. Impact doors are a code requirement and a genuine safety necessity, not just a coastal concern.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to install impact doors in Plantation Acres, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Costs vary based on door type, size, and quantity. A single impact entry door typically runs $2,500–$5,500 installed, while sliding glass doors can range from $4,000–$10,000+. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87/month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners. Many clients find that insurance savings partially offset monthly financing costs, making the net investment more manageable than expected. Contact us for a free, no-obligation estimate tailored to your home.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors in Plantation Acres, and who handles that?
faqs.a: Yes — all exterior door replacements in Plantation Acres require a permit through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf, including submitting product approvals, installation documentation, and scheduling required inspections. We never skip the permit process. Unpermitted work can void insurance claims and complicate future home sales, so proper permitting is non-negotiable on every project we complete.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take in Plantation Acres?
faqs.a: Most single-door replacements are completed in one day. Larger projects involving multiple entry, sliding glass, and French doors across an entire Plantation Acres estate may take two to three days. The permit process typically adds one to two weeks before installation can begin. Once your permit is approved and your product is scheduled, our in-house crew coordinates directly with you to minimize disruption. We never leave a project incomplete between visits — your home remains secure every night.

faqs.q: Will impact doors actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases significantly. Florida law requires insurers to offer wind mitigation discounts for verified impact-rated openings. After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing completes your installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your upgraded doors for your carrier. Many Plantation Acres homeowners see annual premium reductions of $500–$1,500 or more depending on their policy, carrier, and the scope of their impact protection. We provide all NOA documentation and permit records needed for a successful wind mitigation inspection.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Plantation Acres Home with Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Doors?
cta_body: Get a free, no-obligation estimate from Broward County's trusted impact door specialists — The Kings of Service are ready to secure your home with the best-rated products, fully permitted installations, and financing that fits your budget.
